claim: Long-running coding agents require a harness that coordinates multiple context windows with shared artifacts (feature list, progress file, git history) to avoid context overflow
actionable: Build agent harnesses with: (1) feature list JSON tracking test cases, (2) claude_progress file for inter-session state, (3) git commits after each session for checkpoint recovery.
source_title: I Forced Claude to Code for 24 Hours NONSTOP, Here's What Happened

claim: Test-driven development is the foundation of reliable autonomous coding; define all success criteria and test cases upfront before implementation begins
actionable: Create a detailed feature/test list before spawning coding agents; use this as the shared specification that all agents check against.
source_title: I Forced Claude to Code for 24 Hours NONSTOP, Here's What Happened

claim: Regression testing in each agent loop is critical; verify previously-passing features still work before implementing new ones to catch cascading failures early
actionable: In each coding agent session, run regression tests on recently-implemented features before adding new ones; mark broken features as false and fix them immediately.
source_title: I Forced Claude to Code for 24 Hours NONSTOP, Here's What Happened

claim: Claude agents can achieve 50%+ feature completion on complex applications (Claude.ai clone) in 24 hours with proper harness structure; quality improves as session count increases
actionable: Use long-running agents for MVP development; expect 50%+ feature completion for complex UIs when using Opus 4.5 with proper validation.
source_title: I Forced Claude to Code for 24 Hours NONSTOP, Here's What Happened

claim: Browser automation (Puppeteer) validation is essential for long-running coding agents to visually verify features work as intended, not just pass unit tests
actionable: Integrate Puppeteer MCP or similar browser automation into your coding agent harness to validate UI/UX features beyond code-level tests.
source_title: I Forced Claude to Code for 24 Hours NONSTOP, Here's What Happened

claim: Use Claude subscription tokens instead of API keys for long-running agents to control costs; API usage on Opus 4.5 for 24-hour runs is extremely expensive
actionable: For long-running agent experiments, authenticate with Claude desktop token (claude setup-token) rather than API key to avoid massive billing surprises.
source_title: I Forced Claude to Code for 24 Hours NONSTOP, Here's What Happened

claim: The Claude agent SDK enables programmatic control of Claude agents with granular security (sandboxed bash command whitelist, scoped file operations, restricted MCP tools)
actionable: Use the Claude agent SDK (not just CLI) to define custom agents with fine-grained permissions: project directory scope, approved bash commands, specific MCP servers.
source_title: I Forced Claude to Code for 24 Hours NONSTOP, Here's What Happened
